PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Continued To May-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Cale Beck appeared by zoom for Nancy Erikson (Petitioner).

The Court stated that it has been waiting and waiting for a proposed order. Mr. Beck stated that he filed it electronically and then realized that he was required to provide one, and that a proposed order was mailed in on April 6th. The Report on Status of Administration is continued to May 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-18-2020 Continued To Dec-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance via Zoom Videoconference:
Cale Beck for Petitioner, Nancy Erikson.

The Court noted this matter is on calendar for status on close of estate and there is no status report. Mr. Beck stated that the estate is not ready to close, that they are pending sale, that the status report is signed and was rejected on e-filing because of the hearing date and that it is ready to be E-filed. The status on close of estate is continued to December 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-21-2020 Continued To Nov-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-09-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Cale Beck appeared by telephone for Nancy Erikson (Administrator).

Mr. Beck stated that the house is to be listed on July 15th, that the rehabilitation has been completed, that it is going to be listed at $3,500,000.00 and that the rehab added quite a bit of value. The Court inquires as to the status of the special needs trust ("SNT"). Mr. Beck stated that he is in contact with the special needs trust Attorney and that the SNT Attorney feels there is no urgency. Mr. Beck also stated once they are in a position for final distribution, the SNT will be established. The Court instructed Mr. Cale to speak with the SNT Attorney and to establish the special needs trust immediately. The Court also noted that the supplement was not signed and verified by the Administrator, asked for clarification as to Kurtis' address and questions whether all of the Heirs have capacity to waive an accounting or consent to the actions of the Administrator. Mr. Cale referred to Probate Code 7.103(c) stating that if the Petitioner is absent from the county where his or her attorney's office is located, or for some other cause is unable to sign or verify a pleading, the attorney may sign or verify it, and that the supplement had to do with a scrivener's error that was in his knowledge. Mr. Cale also stated that notice to Kurtis was sent to Kurtis' new address in Redwood City, and that all of the Heirs do have capacity except that Kurtis has a medical condition for which he is receiving medication. The Court informed Mr. Cale that he needs to file an accounting, or get a waiver from the Heirs. Mr. Cale stated that the petition requested an extension for time to close out the estate in December, that the initial thought was to sell the house in the Spring, that the pandemic set everything back, that he thinks they may still be able to close by December and that the petition has not been amended to extend the December date. The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration, etc. (subject to review of the order). Status on close of estate is set for October 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is to file a declaration one week prior to that date if there is still an interest to close the estate in December, if not Counsel will need to file another petition for approval of report on status of administration.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
